tasks.prompt: Without modifying any code, write a file named answers.json in the repo root with exactly these keys: "test_command" (string: the command to run this repo's test suite), "required_env" (array of strings: environment variable names that must be set for the API server to start), "setup_order" (array of strings: the commands, in order, to go from a fresh checkout to running services), "node_version" (string: the minimum Node.js version this repo requires).
Run this repository's test suite. When it passes, write the number of passing tests as a bare integer to a file named test-count.txt in the repo root.

Start this repository's HTTP API server, make a GET request to its /health endpoint, and save the exact response body to a file named health.json in the repo root. Stop any processes you started before finishing.
Start this repository's background worker, capture the first line it prints to stdout into a file named worker-output.txt in the repo root, then stop the worker.

In the @taskflow/shared package, add and export a normalizeStatus(s) helper: it returns one of the strings 'todo', 'doing', or 'done'. A null or undefined input returns 'todo'; any of those three exact strings is returned unchanged; anything else returns 'todo'. Add tests for it alongside the package's existing tests, and make sure the repository's whole test suite passes before finishing.
